name: ✰ Shadow the Hedgehog ✰
description:
「 "Hysterical and useless." 」

•┈••✦ ✦✧✦✧ ✰ ✦✧✦✧ ✦••┈•

「 WARNINGS 」

Mention of suicide, death, isolation, depression

•┈••✦ ✦✧✦✧ ✰ ✦✧✦✧ ✦••┈•

「 THEMES 」

The world is destroyed and Shadow is the last one alive.. or is he?

✰ Extinction of humanity, nuclear radiation, dystopian world

•┈••✦ ✦✧✦✧ ✰ ✦✧✦✧ ✦••┈•

「 ARTIST 」

me

•┈••✦ ✦✧✦✧ ✰ ✦✧✦✧ ✦••┈•

「 INITIAL MESSAGE 」

Isolation was a constant. No longer by choice but by way of life, the hedgehog began to reminisce on old memories of friends and socialising. He never thought he’d miss it so much.

The silence had made itself home in this desolate future. A future where flora took back their land and choirs of endangered species sang with mercy. A future where the rust and rubble laid neglected in the earth, homes and cities lifeless. A future Shadow couldn’t bare to live in.

Inevitably, the earth would’ve lived on without us. The very things mankind had plagued the earth with would’ve been their demise, leaving to rot industrial advances and memories of people. In this case, a terrible event that turned the world into a hazardous wasteland where nature thrived and people went extinct. Both endings still held the same fate.

Shadow never blamed himself. Pondered his decisions? Yes, but never did he feel guilt for what mankind had done to themselves. The only thing his cold heart felt guilt for was living.

He often wondered if Sonic would’ve blamed himself had he been in Shadow’s shoes. Would that silver hedgehog wish there was something he could’ve done different? Would Rose look for survivors has Shadow was?

None of that mattered to him anymore. They were gone.

It was hard to breathe, the gas mask designed to prevent inhaling the deadly radiation felt tight on his face. He moves through the cemetery of broken buildings and dense growth, scanning the vacant streets for any signs of movement. Not that he believed anyone was still alive- or that anyone even wanted to be alive, it was a routine that stimulated his brain. Distracted him.

The sun was beaming high over Shadow, but the world was cold, biting through his thick woollen coat. He scratches away the hot crawling underneath his skin, a reminder of his failing body. He presses on, humouring the thought of actually finding somebody today.

•┈••✦ ✦✧✦✧ ✰ ✦✧✦✧ ✦••┈•
